our planet was radically different in almost
every way from the one we live on now.
our planet was radically different in almost every way from the one we live on now.

Erupting volcanoes built up islands
of lava and ash in the global seas.
Erupting volcanoes built up islands of lava and ash in the global seas.

The atmosphere was filled with gases such
as ammonia, methane, hydrogen and steam.
The atmosphere was filled with gases such as ammonia, methane, hydrogen and steam.

There was virtually no oxygen.
In consequence, there was no ozone layer.
There was virtually no oxygen. In consequence, there was no ozone layer.

So ultraviolet rays in strengths that would
be lethal to us bathed the young planet.
So ultraviolet rays in strengths that would be lethal to us bathed the young planet.

The ultraviolet light, together with heat,
electrical and radioactive discharges,
The ultraviolet light, together with heat, electrical and radioactive discharges,

brought about chemical
changes in the waters.
brought about chemical changes in the waters.

including amino acids,
the building blocks of protein.
including amino acids, the building blocks of protein.

For millions of years,
the chemical soup thickened and changed.
For millions of years, the chemical soup thickened and changed.

Possibly some compounds
were added to it from outer space.
Possibly some compounds were added to it from outer space.

Some carbon compounds
aggregated in droplets,
Some carbon compounds aggregated in droplets,

with a membrane
through which other chemicals could pass.
with a membrane through which other chemicals could pass.

Eventually, unusually large molecules appeared
which had extraordinary characteristics.
Eventually, unusually large molecules appeared which had extraordinary characteristics.

They caused amino acids to form
around them, and so built proteins.
They caused amino acids to form around them, and so built proteins.

They could also produce
copies of themselves.
They could also produce copies of themselves.

Such a molecule, known as DNA,
is at the center of every life cell.
Such a molecule, known as DNA, is at the center of every life cell.

Its shape is a double spiral, linked
by chemical units of just four kinds.
Its shape is a double spiral, linked by chemical units of just four kinds.

Their arrangement acts as a code
for the production of proteins,
Their arrangement acts as a code for the production of proteins,

and a group of them in a section of DNA
is called a gene.
and a group of them in a section of DNA is called a gene.

Each half then attracts the correct chemical
units and forms two new, identical molecules.
Each half then attracts the correct chemical units and forms two new, identical molecules.

When this first happened,
primitive cells formed new cells
When this first happened, primitive cells formed new cells
and life on earth had appeared.
and life on earth had appeared.

But sometimes there is
a mistake, a mutation.
But sometimes there is a mistake, a mutation.
These caused variations in the first cells,
These caused variations in the first cells,
and natural selection sorted them out.
and natural selection sorted them out.

Those that were as well or better suited
to their environment survived.
Those that were as well or better suited to their environment survived.
And so, over tens of millions of years,
And so, over tens of millions of years,

a variety of bacteria-like organisms developed,
thrived and invaded new environments on earth.
a variety of bacteria-like organisms developed, thrived and invaded new environments on earth.

We can get a glimpse of what those
first stirrings of life were like
We can get a glimpse of what those first stirrings of life were like

in the hot volcanic springs of such places
as Yellowstone Park in Wyoming.
in the hot volcanic springs of such places as Yellowstone Park in Wyoming.

And in these springs,
staining them a whole variety of colors,

Microorganisms that look to be almost identical

Microorganisms that look to be almost identical to some of the earliest fossils we know.

the water is hottest.
Tufts of bacteria grow where the water is hottest.

silica in strange-colored crusts.
In cooler areas, other bacteria deposit silica in strange-colored crusts.

These bacteria represent the next big step.

For they are probably very like

For they are probably very like the first forms to manufacture food

inside their own cell walls with the
help of energy from the sun: Light.
inside their own cell walls with the help of energy from the sun: Light.

One of the raw materials
they needed was hydrogen.
One of the raw materials they needed was hydrogen.

At first, they got it as sulphuretted
hydrogen, which occurs in volcanic gases.
At first, they got it as sulphuretted hydrogen, which occurs in volcanic gases.

And there are such bacteria flourishing

But then that link with
volcanoes was broken.
But then that link with volcanoes was broken.

Some forms of bacteria arose
which got their hydrogen
Some forms of bacteria arose which got their hydrogen

from a much more widespread
and easily available source: Water.
from a much more widespread and easily available source: Water.

That was a crucial stage
in the history of life.
That was a crucial stage in the history of life.

Because if you take hydrogen from water,
you are left, as a by-product, with oxygen.
Because if you take hydrogen from water, you are left, as a by-product, with oxygen.

These new blue-green bacteria,
or cyanophytes, still exist.
These new blue-green bacteria, or cyanophytes, still exist.

As slime on wet rocks
or in ponds covered with silver bubbles.
As slime on wet rocks or in ponds covered with silver bubbles.

It was they that first contributed oxygen

It was they that first contributed oxygen in large quantities to the atmosphere.

Under the microscope, you can see that they're very simple structures.

to form these great pillars.
they grow large and huge to form these great pillars.

What makes this place so special is that the mouth of the bay

is almost blocked by a bar of sand covered with sea grass.

with the result that these waters are extremely salty.

that we saw on the shores of Lake Superior.

Now, life had reached

Outwardly, things changed little

But eventually, the stage was set
